title: Future Founder Curriculum — Project
description: One quarterly Project containing an ordered array of Lessons. This is the atomic file the app ingests: projects are milestones, lessons are the sequence nodes.

id.description: Convention: {pillarcode}-{ageband}-q{n}. Pillar codes: th,co,bu,le,cw,ex,lw,cr. e.g. cw-13-14-q1

pillars_supporting.description: Keep to <=3. If a project touches every pillar, it is unfocused.

developmental_frame.description: Markdown. What is cognitively/emotionally/socially REAL at this age. Cite the capability, not the aspiration.

authentic_value_meaning.description: Markdown. What 'creating value' genuinely means at THIS age. A lemonade stand and a startup are different acts, not one scaled.

lever.description: The specific psychological driver. 'Make it fun' is not a lever.

mechanism.description: Markdown. How this project's design actually pulls that lever.

artifact.description: The tangible portfolio thing the child ends with (a live offer, a published piece, a working demo).

success_criteria.description: Observable statements a PARENT could verify. No vanity metrics.

concept.description: Markdown. STEP 1 — learn the concept. Tight, age-honest.

real_example.description: Markdown. STEP 2 — a concrete real-world example the child can picture.

apply_task.description: Markdown. STEP 3 — apply it, advancing the project artifact directly.

feedback_task.description: Markdown. STEP 5 — get feedback and improve.

teach_back_task.description: Markdown. STEP 6 — teach someone else. Weighted heavily: this is where retention lives.

ai_judgment_note.description: Markdown. The DURABLE skill: when to reach for AI in this lesson and — more important — when NOT to (struggle first, verify, think unaided). Tools named here are disposable examples.

assets_needed.description: What the app must supply: printables, images, templates, a worksheet.
